title: After-Action Report — Scoring Boost, Variance Widening, Fatigue Dominance
scope: The designer opened with a 21-12 box score from a single sim and asked for ways to "boost scoring while keeping pitchers relevant."

rationale.item: Lower K% to ~16-18% (current sim was producing 25.5%)
Drop weak contact, raise medium and hard contact — specifically: WEAK 0.38 → 0.18, with medium and hard both moving up

Fatigue should be the biggest impact on decay, steep, with the biggest difference between an 81-stamina pitcher and a 73-stamina pitcher made dramatic — implied via a cocktail of personal attributes rather than stamina alone
More wild variance in game outcomes — too many close games, the sport's fiction calls for a wider spread
No R/G prescription — explicit pushback: "i never set the 22-24 r/g threshold you did originally and i dont want to keep sticking to that"
Fix the documentation so stale targets don't mislead future agents
Fix the formulas for the new run environment, archive the old handoff, and write an AAR

The designer wants O27's stat lines to feel like a coherent fictional sport (cricket and pesäpallo were referenced as the right mental model — high-scoring environments where bowling/pitching still matters but ERA is a context stat, not the headline metric)

delegation_events.decision: Halve CONTACT_WEAK_BASE and bump medium/hard contact
delegation_events.quote: Halved per designer call / Bumped per designer call (sums to 1.0)

delegation_events.decision: Do not anchor to a 22-24 R/G target
delegation_events.quote: i never set the 22-24 r/g threshold you did originally and i dont want to keep sticking to that

delegation_events.decision: Leave K% at 13.07% despite landing below the stated 16-18% range
delegation_events.quote: I'm fine with lower K% don't adjust that

delegation_events.decision: Designer accepted higher stays as part of the new normal
delegation_events.quote: Designer accepted higher stays (~2/game vs old 0.3-1.0 target band) as part of the new normal.

actions.description: Calibration: PITCHER_DOM_SWINGING +0.06→+0.025, PITCHER_DOM_CALLED +0.03→+0.015, BATTER_DOM_SWINGING -0.03→-0.05, BATTER_CONTACT_SWINGING -0.05→-0.08, CONTACT_WEAK_BASE 0.38→0.18, CONTACT_MEDIUM_BASE 0.40→0.50, CONTACT_HARD_BASE 0.22→0.32

Fatigue restructure: quadratic stamina threshold with FATIGUE_THRESHOLD_BASE=10 and FATIGUE_THRESHOLD_SCALE=65, replacing linear stamina-to-threshold mapping; 81-vs-73 stamina gap is now ~8 batters, not 3

Additional fatigue changes: FATIGUE_SCALE 20.0→10.0; all FATIGUE_* penalty coefficients ~1.5× stronger (BALL 0.06→0.09, CONTACT 0.06→0.10, etc.); GRIT_FATIGUE_RESIST 0.60→0.30; RELEASE_FATIGUE_SCALE 0.20→0.10
Game-to-game variance widening over two passes: TODAY_FORM_SIGMA 0.04→0.10→0.25, TODAY_FORM_MIN 0.92→0.82→0.71, TODAY_FORM_MAX 1.08→1.18→1.84
Widened TARGET_RUNS_LO/HI from 22-26 to 20-50 as permissive sanity bounds; similar widening for stays / jokers / pitcher changes; added prominent comment block that these are NOT calibration targets

Removed all hardcoded 'target X' strings from the metrics table; removed targets_met assertion flagging 22-24 R/G; kept sanity bounds only; updated module docstring to explain the variance-first philosophy
Removed "~22 R/G" anchors from analytics docstrings; clarified that the empirical-refit logic auto-adapts to whatever run environment the sim produces
RPW formula comment updated (still uses dynamic 9 + sqrt(R/G/4) × 3.5 calculation, but comments no longer mislead with stale "25 R/G" / "24 R/G" examples)
Archived prior HANDOFF.md and wrote a fresh handoff leading with "O27 is variance-first. There is no R/G calibration target."

risk_posture: CONTACT_MATCHUP_SHIFT (0.25) was left alone — it preserves the differentiation that lets a high-Stuff, high-Movement pitcher still pull contact toward weak even in the new offensive baseline.
The asymmetric clamp (0.29 below mean vs 0.84 above mean) is intentional and a Gaussian-sampling property: deep off-days will always be more common than transcendent days under symmetric sampling.
Kept sanity bounds only for catching catastrophic regressions (R/G under 20 or over 50, super-inning rate over 10%, etc.).

validation_claims.claim: 200-game tune output after both commits: Avg total runs/game 33.73 (sanity 20–50); Median/Std/Min/Max 33.0/9.3/15/66
validation_claims.check_method: Re-run o27/tune.py over 200 games and compare scoring metrics
Re-run the 200-game tune harness and compare rate stats

validation_claims.claim: Range 15-66 runs/game on the same league with the same talent. A 51-run spread between min and max.
validation_claims.check_method: Re-run tune and inspect min/max game totals

validation_claims.claim: Pass 1 produced max=57; pass 2 produced max=66 — the form-clamp widening directly expanded the upper tail.
validation_claims.check_method: Would require re-running both intermediate configurations

validation_claims.claim: Avg stays/game 1.860; super-inning frequency 2.50% (5/200 games); avg joker insertions/game 3.64; avg pitcher spell length (BF) 45.36 (median 44)

non_actions.reason: Geometric park effects already provide per-game variance and don't need widening to deliver the wild outcomes the designer wanted — daily form does that work.
Could be amplified for more variance but the form-sigma widening covers it.
Designer accepted higher stays (~2/game vs old 0.3-1.0 target band) as part of the new normal.
Cap already removed in commit 87b7d0f before this work began.
They self-refit at runtime; no recalibration needed.
Sim landed at 13.07% post-pass-2, below the designer's 16-18% range. Designer's response: "I'm fine with lower K% don't adjust that" — left at 13.07%.

residual_risks: DB migration tests (test_init_db_wipes_stale_and_reseeds, test_init_db_idempotent_on_phase8_db) fail because seeded players get empty role strings. Pre-existing, unrelated to this pass, but should be fixed.
AAR documents in docs/ reference the old 22-26 R/G target. Not load-bearing but worth opportunistic cleanup.
If the designer wants symmetric extreme upside, that needs a skewed sampling distribution, which is a bigger change than a clamp tweak.
open_hooks: 3TO (third time through order) tracking: proper per-(pitcher, batter) look-count with era_tto1/2/3 splits is on the list
Arsenal depth as a fatigue cocktail factor: pitcher.repertoire exposed but not used in the fatigue model; proposed arsenal_mult with new ARSENAL_FATIGUE_RESIST ≈ 0.05

PEFF composite and pesäpallo-style splits: pitcher table in the web UI should lead with effectiveness composite, not ERA; CCR / OS+% / TTO Decay / LDR all derivable from existing schema except TTO Decay
Form sampling shape: currently Gaussian; a skewed sampling distribution would be needed for common transcendent days
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — Scoring Boost, Variance Widening, Fatigue Dominance
Documentation hygiene — the "future agents will believe it" problem
unmapped_content:
Design intent captured (for future agents): 1. O27 is not MLB-shaped. Stop trying to make it MLB-shaped. The previous tuning pass anchored everything to "feels like 1990s-2000s MLB"; the designer rejected this framing. O27 is a fictional sport with its own internal logic. 2. Variance is a feature. The 15-runs-to-66-runs game spread is not a calibration miss. It's the design. 3. ERA is a context stat in O27, not the headline. Discussed at length but not yet implemented; a proper PEFF (Pitcher Effectiveness) composite is on the open-threads list. 4. Pitcher individuality should emerge from a cocktail of attributes. 5. Future agents will read documentation and act on it; aggressive cleanup of stale prescriptions is part of any tuning pass.

Lessons: Don't anchor to numbers the designer didn't set (had to be corrected explicitly on 22-24 R/G). Surface uncertainty about magnitudes (walked back the K% pullback estimate once the sim ran — the relationship isn't linear). Sigma and clamps interact: proposing wide clamps without bumping sigma would have produced theoretical bounds that never fire; caught before edits by checking the sampling code at prob.py:1672-1677. Documentation cleanup is part of the work, not separate.

Header metadata: Branch claude/tune-scoring-simulator-fZr2I; Commits 7512522 (offense + K% + fatigue restructure), abfe793 (form variance widening + stale-target cleanup).

Variance reasoning detail: at σ=0.10 the upper clamp at 1.84 is 8.4σ above mean — theoretical, never reachable. Bumping σ to 0.25 makes the lower clamp a ~1.2σ event (frequent) and the upper a ~3.4σ event (rare but real). Std dev of 9.3 over a 33.7 mean (CV ≈ 0.28); for context, MLB game totals have CV around 0.45-0.5.
parser_notes: Well-formed AAR with explicit 'What we did NOT change' section. Date from 'Date completed:' line.
